title: Critoniopsis heydeana
intro: Critoniopsis heydeana, also known as "Lingua de Vaca," is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Critoniopsis heydeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.

Critoniopsis heydeana belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae. More specifically, it falls under the tribe Anthemideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology.
